fingerprint: The dome's 20.5 arcsecond aberration constant IS v_orbital/c (29.78/299,792 km/s = 9.94e-5 rad = 20.49 arcsec) — Earth's orbital velocity ratio. The parameter alpha = 2.56e-8 has no derivation from dome geometry and was reverse-engineered from this globe quantity. The effect's annual sinusoidal period is the orbital period of Earth around the Sun, which has no independent origin on a flat disc.

globe_values.name: Earth's orbital velocity / speed of light
globe_values.source: Bradley (1728); IAU 2009 System of Astronomical Constants; Gaia DR3 astrometric pipeline

dome_values.name: Aberration constant claimed
dome_values.note: Matches v_orbital/c to stated precision. No independent dome derivation exists — no formula connects disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), or any other dome parameter to this value.

dome_values.name: Refractive index alpha
dome_values.note: Asserted without derivation. No dome source file contains a formula path dome_geometry → alpha → aberration_angle. The value appears in a single static one-liner in the WIN list.

match_precisions.dome_aberration_vs_v_over_c: 0.02% (20.5 vs 20.4955 arcsec — the dome rounds the globe value)

why_it_matters: This is a three-layered globe fingerprint: (1) VALUE: The aberration constant 20.5 arcsec equals v_orbital/c to the precision stated — this quantity only has meaning if Earth orbits the Sun at ~30 km/s. On a flat disc with no orbital motion, there is no velocity that produces this ratio. (2) PERIODICITY: Stellar aberration varies sinusoidally with a period of exactly one sidereal year. The dome claims a static refractive index (alpha = constant), but a static medium cannot produce time-varying apparent displacements. The annual period is the period of Earth's orbit — the dome must either invoke a time-varying aether (which requires an annual mechanism indistinguishable from orbital motion) or admit it has no explanation for the periodicity. (3) NO DERIVATION: The dome's evolution log shows WIN-016 appeared in v48.0, approximately 298 years after the aberration constant was first measured. No formula in any dome source file connects dome geometric parameters to alpha = 2.56e-8. The parameter was chosen to reproduce a pre-existing globe measurement, not derived from dome physics.

deeper_analysis.static_medium_paradox: The dome claims alpha is a constant property of the aetheric medium. But aberration is not a constant deflection — it traces an annual ellipse (circular for ecliptic pole stars, linear for ecliptic stars). This requires the deflection direction to rotate with a one-year period. For a static, radially-symmetric dome medium n(r), there is no geometric mechanism to produce a direction-dependent, annually-rotating deflection. The only way to get annual variation from a static medium is to have the observer (or the medium itself) undergo annual motion — which reintroduces orbital motion through the back door.
deeper_analysis.alpha_reverse_engineering: If we assume the dome's formula is something like 'aberration angle = alpha × [geometric factor]', we can test: alpha × disc_radius(km) = 2.56e-8 × 20,015 = 5.12e-4 rad = 105 arcsec (too large by 5×). alpha × firmament_height(km) = 2.56e-8 × 9,086 = 2.33e-4 rad = 48 arcsec (too large by 2.3×). alpha × sun_altitude(km) = 2.56e-8 × 5,733 = 1.47e-4 rad = 30 arcsec (too large by 1.5×). No simple product of alpha with any dome parameter yields 20.5 arcsec. The most likely origin: alpha was chosen so that some undisclosed formula reproduces the known answer. Without a published derivation, this is untestable and unfalsifiable.
deeper_analysis.achromatic_constraint_as_globe_signature: The achromaticity of aberration is itself a globe fingerprint. On a globe, aberration arises from velocity addition (observer velocity / c), which is wavelength-independent by construction. For a refractive mechanism, the Kramers-Kronig relations guarantee dispersion — any physical medium with n > 1 must have wavelength-dependent n. VLBI (GHz) and optical (~500 THz) measurements confirm identical aberration across 6 orders of magnitude in frequency. The achromaticity is a prediction of the globe model that the refractive model cannot reproduce without violating causality.

suggested_text: The aberration constant 20.5 arcsec is simply v<sub>orbital</sub>/c — the ratio of Earth's orbital velocity (29.78 km/s) to the speed of light, a quantity that exists only if Earth orbits the Sun. The dome provides no derivation connecting its parameter alpha = 2.56×10<sup>−8</sup> to any dome geometric feature; the value was adopted nearly 300 years after Bradley's measurement. More damaging still: aberration varies sinusoidally with a period of exactly one sidereal year, but a static refractive medium cannot produce time-varying deflections. The dome must either invoke a time-varying aether — requiring an annual mechanism indistinguishable from orbital motion — or leave the periodicity unexplained.

code_search.found_in: inject_ai_layer.py (confirmed_wins array): {"id": "WIN-016", "title": "Annual aberration refractive model", "data_source": "V48", "year": 2026, "observed": "alpha = 2.56e-8", "status": "confirmed"}. Also in predictions.js as identical static entry.
code_search.notes: Comprehensive search of all 50+ .py/.js files in repo. Value 2.56e-8 exists ONLY as a hardcoded string literal in the confirmed_wins arrays of inject_ai_layer.py and predictions.js. No formula, no computation, no derivation function connects dome parameters (disc_radius, firmament_height, n(r), etc.) to this value. monitor.py has zero mentions of aberration, alpha, or WIN-016 — the claim is never validated by the monitoring infrastructure. wins.html claims the value was 'derived' but no derivation exists anywhere in the repository. The model.html page defines n(r) but never connects it to aberration. update_optical_caveats.py mentions 'aetheric refraction' but only in context of sun altitude optical illusion, not aberration. verify_predictions.py and recalc_v51.py contain no aberration-related content. CONCLUSION: alpha = 2.56e-8 is a naked assertion with zero computational support — the word 'derived' on wins.html is unsupported by any code in the repository.
